Tool_loan_against_property_Description: LAP eligibility from property value and LTV. Max loan, EMI, and interest.
Tool_loan_against_property_Subtitle: Property value × LTV → max loan & EMI
Tool_loan_against_property_LearnText: A loan against property (LAP) lets you borrow against residential or commercial property without selling it. Lenders cap the loan at a loan-to-value (LTV) percentage — often around 50–60%. Lower LTV means a smaller loan but may improve approval odds.
Tool_loan_against_property_FormulaNote: Max loan = property value × LTV%. EMI uses standard reducing-balance monthly compounding. Actual LTV, rate, and tenure depend on property type, income, and lender policy.
Tool_loan_against_property_LtvHint: Typical LAP LTV is about 50–60%. Banks may offer less on commercial property.
Tool_loan_against_property_ScenarioSubti: Same property and rate, different LTV
Tool_loan_against_property_ExampleStep_3: EMI on ₹27.5L at 10.5% for 10 years (120 months).
Tool_loan_against_property_ExampleStep_4: Total payment = EMI × 120; interest = total − principal.
Tool_loan_against_property_ExampleStep_5: Compare lower vs higher LTV — more loan means higher EMI and interest.
Tool_loan_against_property_Step_1: Enter property market value and LTV%.
Tool_loan_against_property_Step_3: Compute EMI on max loan for the tenure.
Tool_loan_against_property_Step_4: Review total interest and LTV scenarios.
Tool_loan_against_property_Info_Abbrevia: LAP — loan against property / mortgage-backed liquidity
Tool_loan_against_property_Info_Purpose: Estimate how much you can borrow against property and the resulting EMI.
Tool_loan_against_property_Info_WhyKnow: LTV caps and tenure drive both approval size and monthly outflow.
Tool_loan_against_property_Info_RealLife: Business expansion, education, debt consolidation, or large expenses without selling property.
Tool_loan_against_property_Info_Alt_Gold: Pledge gold for a few months.
Tool_loan_against_property_Info_Alt_Home: Buying a home with purchase finance.
For purchase, not liquidity on owned property.
Tool_loan_against_property_Info_Differen: FinYatra shows LTV-based max loan with EMI and clear LTV scenario comparison.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Description: Bike and scooter loan EMI from on-road price and down payment.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_LearnText: Two-wheeler loans finance bikes and scooters after a down payment. On-road price includes ex-showroom, RTO, insurance, and dealer charges. A larger down payment cuts the loan and total interest; shorter tenures raise EMI but save interest.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_FormulaNote: Loan = on-road price − down payment. EMI uses reducing-balance monthly compounding. Dealer offers may bundle insurance or processing fees separately.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_PriceHint: Include RTO, insurance, and other on-road charges if known.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_ScenarioSubtitle: Same loan and rate, different tenures
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_ExampleStep_3: EMI on ₹96,000 at 11% for 3 years (36 months).
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_ExampleStep_5: Shorter tenure raises EMI but cuts interest — compare before signing.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Step_1: Enter on-road price and down payment.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Step_3: Compute monthly EMI and total interest.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Info_Abbreviation: Two-wheeler loan — bike / scooter instalment finance
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Info_Purpose: Estimate EMI, loan amount, and interest for a bike or scooter loan.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Info_WhyKnow: On-road extras and tenure choice change what you actually pay each month.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Info_RealLife: Buying a scooter or motorcycle with dealer or bank finance.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Info_Goal_1: Know financed amount after down payment
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Info_Alt_Emi_Examp: Simple EMI on a known principal.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Info_Alt_Personal_: ₹1 lakh personal loan for a bike.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Info_Differentiati: FinYatra starts from on-road price and down payment with tenure scenarios and a yearly breakdown.
Tool_two_wheeler_loan_Info_Motto: Price the ride, then the EMI.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_Description: Max home loan from salary, FOIR, existing EMIs, rate, and tenure.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_LearnText: Banks estimate how much home loan you can service using FOIR (fixed obligation to income ratio). Eligible EMI is a share of monthly income minus existing EMIs. That EMI is then reversed into a maximum principal at your rate and tenure.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_FormulaNote: Eligible EMI = monthly income × FOIR% − existing EMIs. Max loan uses the reverse EMI formula. Lenders also check credit score, property, and co-applicant income — this is an illustrative FOIR-based estimate.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_ExistingHint: Include all ongoing loan EMIs. Leave 0 if none.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_FoirBody: Income {0} × {1} FOIR − existing EMIs {2} → eligible EMI ≈ {3}.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_ExampleStep_3: Reverse EMI at 8.5% for 20 years to get max principal.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_ExampleStep_4: Sample EMI on that principal should match eligible EMI closely.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_ExampleStep_5: Longer tenure raises max loan; stricter FOIR lowers it.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_Step_1: Compute eligible EMI from income, FOIR, and existing EMIs.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_Step_2: Invert EMI to max principal at rate and tenure.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_Step_3: Show sample EMI on that max loan.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_Info_Abbrevia: Home loan eligibility — FOIR-based max loan estimate
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_Info_Purpose: Estimate the maximum home loan your income can support at a given FOIR.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_Info_WhyKnow: Knowing eligibility before property hunting avoids over-stretching EMI.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_Info_RealLife: First home purchase planning, co-applicant capacity checks, pre-approval prep.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_Info_Alt_Home: You already know the loan amount.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_Info_Differen: FinYatra reverses eligible EMI into max loan with FOIR and tenure scenarios.
Tool_home_loan_eligibility_Info_Motto: Qualify the EMI before the property.
